source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane/Lifting Device - West Cameron 544-A

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - West Cameron Block 544

event.top_event: Projectile Impact Injury from Sand Hopper Lid Failure
actions_taken: Air compressor supply line ball valve closed by crew member
Air Med Helicopter dispatched and arrived at 4:20 PM
Injured person transported to trauma facility in Galveston, Texas
event.summary: On 24-OCT-2014 at approximately 1455 hours during routine sandblast and paint operations on Arena Offshore's West Cameron 544-A facility, the top hatch lid of a sand hopper failed catastrophically due to poor condition of fastening components. The lid dislodged, was deflected by the upper rack assembly, and struck a blast and paint crew member along a nearby walkway, causing significant injuries including head lacerations, finger damage, mandible fracture, broken teeth, and pelvic fracture. Investigation revealed worn bolt threads with inconsistent washers and mismatched nuts, two of which were completely missing. Pre-job inspection procedures and daily maintenance checklists were not properly followed.
recommendations: BSEE Lake Charles District recommends the Office of Safety Management issue a safety alert identifying the hazards associated with sandblasting equipment when not being properly maintained
key_phrases: sand hopper top hatch lid failure

hazards.name: Defective fastening components on sand hopper lid

threats.name: Degradation of bolt threads and fastener integrity
Loss of fastener retention due to worn threads and missing nuts

consequences.name: Emergency evacuation and medical evacuation required

controls.name: Sand Hopper Pre Job Check List
controls.barrier_role: Inspection and verification of equipment condition prior to offshore deployment

supporting_text: On July 10, 2014 a 'Sand Hopper Pre Job Check List' was completed prior to sending the equipment offshore; however, this inspection failed to identify the poor condition of the bolt threads.

controls.name: Crew walk-through equipment inspection procedure
controls.barrier_role: Pre-startup verification that all equipment is in safe working condition
controls.lod_basis: Company procedure requiring crew inspection before operations commence

supporting_text: the third party blast and paint company's pre-job/start up procedure states the 'crew will make walk thru to check all equipment associated with blasting operations, to ensure equipment is in good, safe working condition.'
Failure to follow the company's Best Management Practices Plan

controls.name: Filling Sand Hopper Safe Work Practices - Hatch and fastener check
controls.barrier_role: Verification of proper hatch installation and fastener security before pressurization
controls.lod_basis: Company procedure for safe hopper filling operations

supporting_text: their 'Filling Sand Hopper Safe Work Practices' procedure states to 'replace top hatch to proper position, check cam locks, and bolts/nuts.'

controls.name: Daily inspection of bolts and fasteners - Pressurizing Sand Hopper checklist
controls.barrier_role: Daily verification of bolt tightness and wear condition before pressurization
controls.lod_basis: Company daily maintenance and pre-operation checklist

supporting_text: their written checklist 'Pressurizing Sand Hopper Safe Work Practices/Checklist' states to 'Inspect bolts on hatches for tightness and wear daily. When lid is re-installed, ensure lid is on correctly and cam locks are re-secured evenly.'

controls.name: Best Management Practices (BMP) Plan for blast and paint operations
controls.barrier_role: Documented procedures and standards for safe sandblasting and painting operations

supporting_text: the inspectors requested to see the company's required Best Management Practices (BMP) Plan for blast and paint operations and they were unable to produce the document

controls.name: Formal on-site equipment inspection prior to start-up
controls.barrier_role: Pre-operation verification of equipment condition and safety systems

supporting_text: the platform operator indicated there was not any sort of formal on-site equipment inspection performed prior to start-up

controls.name: Fastener maintenance and replacement program
controls.barrier_role: Preventive maintenance to ensure fastener integrity and proper assembly

supporting_text: the bolt threads associated with the cam-lock fasteners were in poor condition and numerous flattened as well as stripped threads were identified
two of the four nuts were completely missing and one of the two remaining nuts was the wrong type for the application

controls.name: Emergency response and medical evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Rapid response to minimize injury severity and provide emergency medical care

supporting_text: the PIC immediately made phone calls to initiate an emergency response which involved an Air Med Helicopter to fly offshore and transport the IP to the hospital

The Air Med Helicopter arrived on location at 4:20pm and once the Paramedic checked the IP's condition, the crew members loaded him onto a stokes litter and lifted him with the platform's crane up onto the heliport

controls.name: Immediate shutdown of air compressor supply
controls.barrier_role: Rapid isolation of pressurized system to prevent further hazard escalation

supporting_text: one of the blast and paint crew members to respond to the air compressor supplying the sand hopper by closing the ball valve to the supply line, as well as shutting down the unit

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
